PALO ALIO

sAN TnANCISCO, CA 941442675

TELEFACSIMILE (475t 43+7s22
TEIIX 34.0154 MRSN FOERS SFO
January 26 | l99O
DIRXCT DIAL NTJMBER
(4r5) 67 7 -63L2
VIA FEDERAL EXPRESS
California Fair Political Practices Conrnission

428 J Stree
Suite 8oo 
:-l
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 
. .:-
,
Disclosure Provisions 
g
Dear Sirs:
On behalf of our clients, RREEF Anerica Partners
(,,RREEF,,) and RCM Capital Managenent ( "RCM, ) (sometimes each
referred to as an /Adviserf or collectively as the
,,Advisersr'), we are requesting guidance from the Fair
PoIiticaI Practices Cornmission (rFPPC,r) as to the
applicability of revised covernnent Code Section A72oo.
Each Adviser is a Catifornia partnership, and is registered
as an investment adviser with the Securities and Exchange
Commission under the Investment Advisers Act of 1940.
Pursuant to applicable lar^/, each Adviser is required to
provide annual disclosure statements to its clients and to
keep detailed books and records which are available for
examination by regulatory agencies. These books and records
are also available for review by a client of the Adviser
with respect to the particular client,s account activity.
RREEF,s business is lirnited to the managenent of
real estate investment portfolios for institutional
clientele (e.9., corporate and government ernployee benefit
pl-ans). RCM,s business is limited to the management of
securities portfolios for institutional 
clientele and high-
net worth individuals.
RCM is presently a party to an investment
management agreement with the San Francisco City and county


MonnrsoN & Foensrnn
California Fair Political Practices Commi-ssion
January 26, L99O
Page Thro
Retirement System (the ,,System,, ) and RREEF is presently
negotiating the terms of such an agreement with the System.
We note that recently-amended Government Code
Section 872O0 imposes the FPPC Form 72I fir-ing requirements
on rpublic officials 
who nanage pubJ"ic investments, and that
the tern ,,public officials,, is defined in cal. code of
Regs. 518700(a) to include t,consultants,, of a state or local
government agency. Section 187oo(a)(2) of the Regulations
further defines the tern ,,consuItant,,. However, neither the
Forn 721 nor the Regulations appear to have been prepared
with independent investment managers in mind. As a result,
we seek clarification 
from the EPPC as to a number of
matters with respect to the Advisers.
(1) Each Adviser,s authority to acquire and seII
assets on behalf of the System is restricted by the Agency,s
,,Written cuidelines,,, applicable to the particular portfolio
account, which may be amended at will by the System. Is the
definition of ,,consuItant,, interpreted so as to distinguish
between non-discretionary and discretionary investment
management services, or to otherwise recognize some
identifiable leveI of cl,ient invoLvement in the investment
decision-making process? It is oftentimes the case with
real estate nanagers. for exanple, that clients reserve
final authority as to buy-ho1d-sell decisions with respect
to reaf property.
(2) It is clear that the Regulations and the
Form 721 are intended to cover ,rnatural persons,r. As each
actual governnent official 
with filing responsibility is a
natural personr the Form does not take into account the
wholly different character of multi-ernployee investment
management organizations. Where an investment manager is an
entity, who should be the party that files the Form 721?
Should the entity be the filing party, or should an ernployee
or employees file on an individual basis? In the case of
RREEF and RCl,l, each has professional staffs in excess of 25
persons. what if investment advice is provided to an
account through the efforts of numerous natural persons
within the organization, each with varying leveIs and types
of internal respons ibi I ities? 
May one person be designated
as primary manager or client contact for sole filing
purposes ?


MonnrsoN &Fornsrnn
California Fair PoIitical Practices Commission
January 26, l99O
Page Three
(3) The Form 721 refers to the disclosure of
investments held in the ,, jurisdiction,,. 
This concept bears
little 
relation to the actions of an independent investment
manager, who will typically have authority to invest outside
the .. jurisdictiontt of its client. 
Real estate portfolios,
for example, will norrnally be limited at most to a list of
states or metropol-itan areas in various states. with
respect to securities managers, the securities will
typically be acquired on national securities markets and
will be securities of issuers with regional, national or
international operations.
(a) On what basis are securities holdings
considered in Ia] jurisdictiontt? 
Are there tests such as
issuer,s principal place of business, percent of revenue or
income from a particular locale, number of Local employees
relative to world-wide ernployment, or otherwise? I'Iay an
outside manager avoid the disclosure of holdings of
securities of issuers whose securities are Iisted for
trading on a national securities exchange or on the
established NASDAQ over-the-counter market? May a real
estate manager avoid the necessity of any disclosure of
securities holdings?
(b) May disclosure be limited to those assets
hrhich $rould be subject to the ,,material financial effect',
rules standards in Regulation 518702?
(4) Does the fi1ing obligation conlnence within
(a) 10 days, or (b) 30 days of (c) the ,ras of,, date of a
nanagenent agreement, or (d) its actual execution date?
Current rules do not take into account the engagement of
investment management organizations.
( 5 ) Do you intend to promulgate new Regulations
and a separate form for the required filings of independent
investment managers?
(6) May investrnent managers with filing
responsibility seek and obtain confidential treatment for
these filings? 
These are not candidates for or holders of
public office, and no other category of private contractor
to a public agency is subject to the requirement of making a
public filing of personal financial condition. Confidential
treatnent would seem appropriate under these circumstances.
Based on conversations with the FPPC staff, 
we
understand that neither Adviser nor any of their personnel


MonnrsoN & Fonnsrnn
California Fair Political Practices comrnission
January 26 | I99O
Page Four
will be required to make a filing under Section 872O0 until
after you have had the opportunity to respond to this
letter.
We appreciate your attention to these inquiries.
If you need any further inforrnation concerning this request,
please feel free to contact the undersigned or Cary Klafter
of this office (415-677-7LOL).
Very truly yours,
q.\"r*-,.6Gnq_
Elizabeth c. Gentry (J
EGG : jmc
cc: Cary I Klafter, Esq.
Trish Dehn
Richard Raybin
D33538 [ eSSl ]

sections.question_synthetic: The letter requests FPPC guidance on the applicability of revised Government Code Section 87200 and Form 721 filing requirements to independent investment management firms (RREEF America Partners and RCM Capital Management) that manage public investments for the San Francisco City and County Retirement System, with six specific questions regarding consultant definitions, entity vs. individual filing obligations, jurisdiction-based disclosure requirements, filing timeline commencement, potential new regulations, and confidential treatment eligibility.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request/inquiry letter from a law firm to the FPPC, not an FPPC response or advice letter. No CONCLUSION or ANSWER section is present. The document contains detailed QUESTIONS (numbered 1-6) but no FPPC response. The letter references that FPPC staff conversation occurred but the actual FPPC response/advice letter is not included in this document.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: The letter requests FPPC guidance on the applicability of revised Government Code Section 87200 and Form 721 filing requirements to independent investment management firms (RREEF America Partners and RCM Capital Management) that manage public investments for the San Francisco City and County Retirement System, with six specific questions regarding consultant definitions, entity vs. individual filing obligations, jurisdiction-based disclosure requirements, filing timeline commencement, potential new regulations, and confidential treatment eligibility.

embedding.summary: Morrison & Forster law firm submits an incoming request letter to the FPPC on behalf of two investment management firms seeking clarification on whether they qualify as "consultants" subject to Form 721 filing requirements under Government Code Section 87200, and requesting guidance on six specific implementation questions regarding disclosure obligations for independent investment managers.
